About Grant Cardone
• 	Grant Cardone is a New York Times bestselling author of five books since the 2008 economic collapse.
He is an international speaker, business innovator and leading social media personality in the world
today. He also provides business articles for Entrepreneur Mag, Wells Fargo, Business Insider, AMEX
Open, Business Insider, Huffington Post and more.
• 	Grant Cardone grew up in a middle class family in Louisiana. He was broke at 25 and by the age of 30 he
had banked his first million dollars with Cardone Enterprises. Today, Cardone is the CEO of four privately
held companies in technology, consulting, real estate and digital ecommerce that produce almost $100m
in sales. His first partnership was in 1989 and is still thriving today. Cardone Acquisition, a Real Estate
company, started in 1993 has conducted business in 8 different states and has an accumulated $500m in
transactions. Grant Cardone TV is his newest venture and operates from Miami Beach.
• 	Grant Cardone is married to Elena Cardone and has two children, Sabrina and Scarlett.
• 	Favorite quotes – “One day I’m going to make it ‘til then I am going to hustle.”

Grant Cardone’s Story
“I grew up middle class. My dad was the first educated in his family and it was his dream to create a middle class lifestyle for his five kids and
wife. The middle class was a dream my dad made a reality. My dad started as a paymaster (accountant) for the US Army, sold automobiles for
a short stint, owned a neighborhood grocery store, life insurance sales and was a stock broker. My dad started having heart problems at the
age of 42 the same year my twin brother and I were born. At the age of 50, my dad bought his dream house and less than two years later he
was dead.
My mother was left with a house she couldn’t take care of, no way to create new income and a little bit of life insurance money. Everyday she
was reminded we were at financial risk. Money going out and no money coming in. I could see and hear the fear my mother was experiencing
and at the age of 10 I couldn’t do much about it. My mom would always tell us how well my dad had done to take care of us and how grateful
we should be to have a middle class life. At the age of 16 I had enough and told my mom, “this middle class thing is a joke–when I grow up I
am going to be rich and if I get there I am going to help a lot of people.” My mom thought I was an unappreciative, disrespectful teenager–
which was probably true–but looking back now, one of the primary missions and purposes of my life was being formed.
By the time I was 31, I had saved my first million dollars. Over the last 25 years I have built four companies that have allowed me to create an
unbelievable lifestyle for myself, my family and employees. Today, the four companies which range from service business to technology to real
estate will do almost $100 million in combined sales and employ almost 200 people in eight states.
While most people know me as a sales and marketing guy, author, speaker and social media expert, the thing I am most proud of is that I am
fulfilling the promise to my mom I made at 16, “if I ever make it I am going to help a lot of people.” So thank you for being willing to receive help
because without you I couldn’t fulfill my mission on this planet.” - GC

18 years in the NFL with the Minnesota Vikings and New York Giants. Three Super Bowl
appearances. 3,686 completions, 6,467 pass attempts, 342 touchdowns, 47,003 yards passing, and
3,674 yards rushing — all NFL records. A Hall of Fame induction in 1986. Monday Night Football.
“That’s Incredible.”
Those are some of the things that might come to your mind when you hear the name Fran Tarkenton.
But if you ask him, he’ll give you a different answer. “I’m an entrepreneur, first and always,” he says.
Over 50 years in business, Tarkenton has built more than 20 successful companies, all from the
ground up. And today, 74 years old, he remains as active and involved as ever, running multiple
companies out of his office in Atlanta, Georgia, including GoSmallBiz.com, Tarkenton Financial and
SmallBizClub.com.
About Fran 	Tarkenton

Grant Cardone’s 12 Commandments
1. 	Went to market directly.
Because I had no money I made phone calls directly into businesses
and then when I failed on the phone I got on a plane and went to visit
every prospect that said no to me.
This was my competitive advantage because no one else had the
courage or persistence to do this.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 62 of 95 --

Share this E-Book: 	MakeMillionsInBusiness.com 	| 	310.777.0255!
@ GRANTCARDONE
/ GRANTCARDONEFAN
/ GRANTCARDONE
Grant Cardone’s 12 Commandments
2. 	Promoted like a maniac.
No one is going to market you louder that you are. Over commit and
over promise. Promise the world make big claims and then over deliver
on everything you promise.
Scream as loud as you can, make noise constantly and disregard who
you bother. Your goal is to spend nothing on advertising until the world
knows you by first name.

Grant Cardone’s 12 Commandments
3. 	Made sales my Divine Inspiration.
The first and last thing I measured then and now was my revenue. I
made sales my divine inspiration and still today only pay rewards/
bonuses on the ability to assist in a sale or make a sale.
Sales is the lifeblood and oxygen of all business. Spend 95% of your
time on revenue and 5% on expenses.

Grant Cardone’s 12 Commandments
4. 	I did it before I delegated it.
I refused to turn over a task before I did it myself. I wanted to know every
task, every challenge before I started bringing on others to do that task.
This way I could set the bar and know what was reasonable to expect.
No one knows the task better than me and everywhere I neglected this we
have problems.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

Grant Cardone’s 12 Commandments
5. 	Branded before I advertised.
I refused to spend money on advertising until my name had been branded.
This could have been a mistake but I think not. It is my belief that people spend
money to advertise before they spend money to brand.
Spend your money branding your name, your claim and your fame into the
publics brain before you spend a penny on advertising.
Never has it been so easy and so difficult to brand your name into the publics
mind.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

Grant Cardone’s 12 Commandments
6. 	Cash Flow is the Holy Grail.
There is no more important business monitor than cash flow. It is free cash
flow that allows your business to continue to expand and invest. This is the
most overlooked and misunderstood of business indicators.
I protected cash flow like the most important thing in my business second
only to revenue. Companies that measure only profit without measuring
cash flow will soon find they have no profits and no cash flow.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

Grant Cardone’s 12 Commandments
7. 	Created Multiple Parallel Flows.
My second business was a parallel company whereby the first company
could send business and opportunities to the new company.
Look for secondary product flows that could turn into stand alone
companies that can be supported by the primary business.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

Grant Cardone’s 12 Commandments
8. 	Stay Broke.
Burn this into your consciousness, “money doesn’t sleep but it does get bored.”
Money left hanging around will find emergencies. To get a million dollars in a
bank I would throw all free cash into a sacred account every time I got it so that
it would appear that the company and myself were broke.
This inspired me to go out and continue to move away from poverty and get
new customers to fund new activities. Stay broke, stay hungry and if you are
going to have money laying around invest it as fast as you can.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 69 of 95 --

Share this E-Book: 	MakeMillionsInBusiness.com 	| 	310.777.0255!
@ GRANTCARDONE
/ GRANTCARDONEFAN
/ GRANTCARDONE
Grant Cardone’s 12 Commandments
9. 	Keep Hustling.
The moment you quit hustling you will get out hustled. The moment you are busy moving around in the
market you will be forgotten.
You must wake up everyday to out work, out hustle, out maneuver your every competitor. Even long
after you have hit it big stay hustling, branding yourself as someone that will not go away.
The first reason I hustled was because I was scared, the second reason was to set the example for
my competitors, the third reason was to convince myself I was committed and the last reason was for
customers.
Make a list of when your competition will not work it out and what they will not do and go there. There
is not business without busyness – make noise and stay in the market.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 70 of 95 --

Share this E-Book: 	MakeMillionsInBusiness.com 	| 	310.777.0255!
@ GRANTCARDONE
/ GRANTCARDONEFAN
/ GRANTCARDONE
Grant Cardone’s 12 Commandments
10. Beat Up New Technology and Throw It Away.
Jack Welch of GE said, “if you can’t dominate don’t spend your time there.” I love this saying and live by this today.
A few years ago I overheard someone say that one of my competitors hated social media. The next day we were
banging on social media for my life. We exploited every social media I could find because I knew this guy hated new
technology and was fixed in the past. Unfortunately or fortunately this seems to happen to most people.
Like that old man or woman – I don’t have time for that. I don’t want to be that guy that gets old and unwilling to look at
technology developments. I embrace everything that can allow me to scale out and brand and dominate the
competition. Let the companies provide the technology and I will show you how to beat it up and then discard it when
its over.
I trust that my brand, my name and my company will be around longer than 99% of all new developments but that
doesn’t mean I can’t use them while they are around. In all my companies we make lists of all new technologies
available to the company that we can USE not be used by.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 71 of 95 --

Share this E-Book: 	MakeMillionsInBusiness.com 	| 	310.777.0255!
@ GRANTCARDONE
/ GRANTCARDONEFAN
/ GRANTCARDONE
Grant Cardone’s 12 Commandments
11. 	Expand While Others Bitch Out
The biggest move I ever made was when the rest of the market was in retreat. 2009 the market was in
collapse and I turned it on. I couldn’t spend money at the time because everything was frozen but I
invested energy and creativity like I was a billionaire. Warren Buffet says, “be greedy when others are
fearful.”
Remember when you don’t have money you still have commitment, creativity, energy and ideas. Use
those like they are money and expand while others defend.
When others are retreating and playing defense take the field and play offense. When they won’t
spend you must. When everyone is going all out, it might be the time to say, “what?” And be a little
cautious – but when they are terrified and capitulating that is the time when you want to go all in with
your expansion moves.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 72 of 95 --

Share this E-Book: 	MakeMillionsInBusiness.com 	| 	310.777.0255!
@ GRANTCARDONE
/ GRANTCARDONEFAN
/ GRANTCARDONE
Grant Cardone’s 12 Commandments
12. 	Fire to Grow.
When you are unable to grow, fire someone. Seriously. The whole “I don’t want to lose people” is a guarantee
your company will fail. Measure your people on production. If they don’t produce get rid of them. You can’t
build a winning team with losing teammates.
Hire and fire like the NFL. You either produce or you don’t, we either win with you or we don’t. Any time I am
pushing as the commander in chief of my company and things are not going the way I KNOW they should, I
look for who is in my way. That one person that is creating dissention, doubt, and a bad example. There is
always someone doing this.
Be willing to fire to grow. I don’t keep people because they have been with me. I keep people that make the
company better, faster and more profitable.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com
